Reynolds_4_0_Corp
children.description: Manufacturing Operations — regional or divisional grouping. In Walker Reynolds' model this maps to ISA-95 L1 (Enterprise/Site boundary). Represents a cluster of related production sites under common operational management.

children.name: site-a
children.description: Site A — primary manufacturing facility. Walker Reynolds: the site is the 'home' of operational data. All raw signals live here; analytics and ERP subscribe from this tree.

children.name: production
children.description: Production area — main manufacturing lines. ISA-95 L3 / ISA-88 process cell.

children.name: line-01
children.description: Production line 01 — primary product line. ISA-95 L4: independent PLC scope. Walker Reynolds: 'a line is the smallest unit with independent operational control.'

children.name: machine-01
children.description: Primary process machine on line 01. ISA-95 L5 / ISA-88 unit. Walker Reynolds: signals live here, not at the line level.

tags.name: state
tags.description: Machine running state — true = running. Report by exception.

tags.name: fault
tags.description: Fault or stop condition — true = faulted. Report by exception.

tags.name: temperature
tags.description: Process temperature. Unit in payload (Cel), never in topic.

tags.name: oee
tags.description: OEE — computed locally at the asset level, published at the asset topic. Walker Reynolds: derived values live at the node that owns the computation.

children.name: conveyor-01
children.description: Infeed / outfeed conveyor for machine 01. ISA-95 L5.

children.name: line-02
children.description: Production line 02 — secondary product line. ISA-95 L4.

children.name: machine-02
children.description: Primary process machine on line 02.

tags.name: recipe
tags.description: Active recipe — published on change (report by exception).

children.name: utilities
children.description: Utilities area — energy supply and support systems. ISA-95 L3.

children.name: energy
children.description: Energy supply — electricity, steam, compressed air. L4.

children.name: main-meter
children.description: Main site electricity meter. Walker Reynolds: utility meters are assets just like production equipment — same hierarchy, same topic pattern.

children.name: boiler-01
children.description: Steam boiler 01. L5.

children.name: quality
children.description: Quality area — in-line quality measurement and test stations. ISA-95 L3. Walker Reynolds: quality data belongs in the physical hierarchy at the point where it is measured, not in a separate 'quality system' silo.

children.name: inspection
children.description: Inline inspection and end-of-line test. L4.

children.name: vision-system-01
children.description: Machine vision inspection system 01. L5.

tags.name: reject
tags.description: Rejection signal — product rejected by vision check

children.name: lab-station-01
children.description: Inline lab measurement station. Walker Reynolds: even lab instruments are part of the physical hierarchy — they are assets that produce data at a specific location.

tags.name: measurement
tags.description: Primary quality measurement (e.g. concentration, purity). Unit: %.

tags.name: spec-pass
tags.description: Result within specification — true = pass. Report by exception.

tags.name: temperature
tags.description: Sample/process temperature at measurement point. Unit: Cel.

children.name: logistics
children.description: Logistics area — inbound raw material and outbound finished goods. ISA-95 L3. Walker Reynolds: logistics data belongs in the physical namespace alongside production — same topology, same single source of truth.

children.name: inbound
children.description: Inbound raw material receiving — weighbridges and silos. L4.

children.name: weighbridge-01
children.description: Truck weighbridge 01 — inbound gross/tare/net weighing. L5.

tags.name: inbound-tonnage
tags.description: Cumulative inbound raw material tonnage. Unit: t.

children.name: silo-01
children.description: Raw material buffer silo 01. L5.

tags.name: level
tags.description: Silo fill level as percentage. Unit: %.
